ucs.title: [LLM] The White Company (Operation Shaheen) — weaponized Office lure spawning Equation Editor / LOLBin payload chain
ucs.description: Detects the Operation Shaheen initial-access chain where a Pakistani-military-themed RTF/DOC arrives via spearphish and the Office app spawns EQNEDT32.EXE (CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0802) or LOLBins (cmd, mshta, certutil, wscript) that drop NetWire RAT or the Shaheen multi-stage loader. Tied specifically to The White Company's documented exploitation of Equation Editor against Pakistan Air Force and government targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_hash) as process_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE","POWERPNT.EXE","OUTLOOK.EXE") AND (Processes.process_name IN ("EQNEDT32.EXE","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","regsvr32.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*\\AppData\\*\\*.exe","*\\Temp\\*.exe","*ping -n*del*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Email.src_user) as sender values(All_Email.file_name) as attachment from datamodel=Email.All_Email where All_Email.file_name IN ("*.doc","*.docx","*.rtf","*.xls","*.xlsx") AND (All_Email.subject="*Pakistan*" OR All_Email.subject="*PAF*" OR All_Email.subject="*Shaheen*" OR All_Email.subject="*MoD*" OR All_Email.subject="*Inter Services*") by All_Email.recipient | rename All_Email.recipient as dest] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let officeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let badChildren = dynamic(["eqnedt32.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe"]);
let phishLures = EmailAttachmentInfo
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName endswith ".doc" or FileName endswith ".docx" or FileName endswith ".rtf" or FileName endswith ".xls" or FileName endswith ".xlsx"
| join kind=inner (EmailEvents | where Subject has_any ("Pakistan","PAF","Shaheen","Inter Services","MoD","Pak Army","Joint Staff")) on NetworkMessageId
| project Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, FileName, SHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeApps)
| where FileName in~ (badChildren)
   or FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\")
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ping -n", "timeout /t", "choice /t") and ProcessCommandLine has "del "
| join kind=leftouter (phishLures) on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, SHA256, SenderFromAddress, FileName1
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Cylance's Operation Shaheen report documents that The White Company specifically weaponized Equation Editor (CVE-2017-11882) inside RTF lures themed around Pakistan Air Force, ISPR, and Pak Army content delivered to .gov.pk and .mil.pk targets. The OFFICE→EQNEDT32 child-process pattern combined with Pakistani-themed lure subject lines is a near-unique fingerprint of this actor in 2017–2018; boltin

ucs.title: [LLM] The White Company anti-analysis 'AV-aware self-surrender' loader behavior
ucs.description: Hunts the distinctive Operation Shaheen loader behavior that enumerates a hard-coded list of AV products (Kaspersky, ESET, Quick Heal, BitDefender, Sophos, AVG, Avira, Avast), checks system time/date, and then self-deletes via a ping/timeout-then-del batch trick. The actor deliberately allowed certain AVs to detect the binary to frustrate researchers, making this combined enumeration→time-check→self-delete chain a high-signal fingerprint.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_path IN ("*\\Users\\*\\AppData\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_id Processes.parent_process_name _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval av_recon=if(match(cmdline,"(?i)(kaspersky|eset|nod32|quickheal|bitdefender|sophos|avgnt|avastsvc|avgsvc|mcshield|ekrn|avgui)"),1,0) | eval time_check=if(match(cmdline,"(?i)(GetSystemTime|GetLocalTime|net time|w32tm|systeminfo.*BIOS)"),1,0) | eval self_delete=if(match(cmdline,"(?i)(ping(\s+-n\s+\d+\s+127\.0\.0\.1).*del|choice\s+/c\s+y.*del|timeout\s+/t\s+\d+.*del)"),1,0) | stats sum(av_recon) as av_recon sum(time_check) as time_check sum(self_delete) as self_delete values(cmdline) as commands values(proc) as processes by dest parent_process_name parent_process_id | where av_recon>=1 AND (time_check>=1 OR self_delete>=1) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let window = 10m;
let avTerms = dynamic(["kaspersky","avp.exe","ekrn","nod32","egui","quickheal","bitdefender","vsserv","sophos","savservice","avgnt","avgsvc","avastsvc","avastui","avguard","avira","mcshield"]);
let suspiciousParents = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\")
| project DeviceId, DeviceName, ParentPid=InitiatingProcessId, ParentName=InitiatingProcessFileName, Timestamp, ProcessCommandLine, FileName, FolderPath;
suspiciousParents
| extend AvRecon = iff(ProcessCommandLine has_any (avTerms) or FileName =~ "tasklist.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any (avTerms) or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "AntiVirusProduct") or (FileName =~ "reg.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "SecurityCenter2"), 1, 0)
| extend TimeCheck = iff(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("net time","w32tm","systeminfo") or FileName =~ "systeminfo.exe", 1, 0)
| extend SelfDelete = iff(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)ping\s+-n\s+\d+\s+127\.0\.0\.1.*del\s" or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)choice\s+/c\s+\w.*del\s" or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)timeout\s+/t\s+\d+.*del\s", 1, 0)
| summarize AvRecon=max(AvRecon), TimeCheck=max(TimeCheck), SelfDelete=max(SelfDelete), Commands=make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25), Children=make_set(FileName, 25), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName, ParentPid, ParentName, bin(Timestamp, window)
| where AvRecon == 1 and (TimeCheck == 1 or SelfDelete == 1)
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceFileEvents | where ActionType == "FileDeleted" and InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\")) on DeviceId
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Cylance's reverse engineering of the Shaheen loader showed a deliberately unusual anti-analysis routine: enumerate a fixed set of AV products and selectively let some catch the sample, query system time, then self-delete using a ping-loopback-then-del trampoline. Most APTs do AV recon OR self-delete, but the *combination from a binary running out of AppData/Temp* within minutes is highly character
